get-started.title: Get started
get-started.server_title: Set up a server for access on multiple devices

hero_section.title: Organize your thoughts. Build your personal knowledge base.
hero_section.subtitle: Trilium is an open-source solution for note-taking and organizing a personal knowledge base. Use it locally on your desktop, or sync it with your self-hosted server to keep your notes everywhere you go.
hero_section.screenshot_alt: Screenshot of the Trilium Notes desktop application

organization_benefits.title: Structure your notes, your way
organization_benefits.note_structure_description: Notes can be arranged hierarchically. There's no need for folders, since each note can contain sub-notes. A single note can be added in multiple places in the hierarchy.
organization_benefits.attributes_description: Use relations between notes or add labels for easy categorization. Use promoted attributes to enter structured information which can be used in tables and boards.
organization_benefits.hoisting_description: Easily separate your personal and work notes by grouping them under a workspace, which focuses your note tree to only show a specific set of notes.

productivity_benefits.title: Everyday power features
productivity_benefits.revisions_content: Notes are periodically saved in the background and revisions can be used for review or to undo accidental changes. Revisions can also be created on-demand.
productivity_benefits.protected_notes_content: Protect sensitive personal information by encrypting the notes and locking them behind a password-protected session.
productivity_benefits.auth_content: Protect your server login with multi-factor authentication (TOTP), or sign in through your identity provider with OpenID Connect.
productivity_benefits.search_content: Jump to any note or command by title with fuzzy matching, or run a full-text search across your notes, even text inside images and PDFs via OCR.
productivity_benefits.templates_content: Define reusable note templates with a preset structure, attributes and content, then create new notes from them in a click.
productivity_benefits.web_clipper_content: Grab web pages (or screenshots) and place them directly into Trilium using the web clipper browser extension.
productivity_benefits.sync_content: Use a self-hosted or cloud instance to easily synchronize your notes across multiple devices, and to access it from your mobile phone using a PWA.
productivity_benefits.share_content: If you have a server, publish any subtree of your notes as a public, read-only website that anyone can browse without an account.
productivity_benefits.api_content: Read, create and update your notes programmatically over HTTP with Trilium's built-in REST API, ideal for automations and third-party integrations.

note_types.title: A note type for every kind of content
note_types.subtitle: Not everything fits neatly into a paragraph. Trilium offers a range of note types, from rich text and code to spreadsheets, diagrams and mind maps, so you can capture each idea in the form that suits it best and mix them freely throughout your tree.
note_types.text_description: The notes are edited using a visual (WYSIWYG) editor, with support for tables, images, math expressions, code blocks with syntax highlighting. Quickly format the text using Markdown-like syntax or using slash commands.
note_types.code_description: Large samples of source code or scripts use a dedicated editor, with syntax highlighting for many programming languages and with various color themes.
note_types.file_description: Embed multimedia files such as PDFs, images, videos with an in-application preview.
note_types.canvas_description: Arrange shapes, images and text across an infinite canvas, using the same technology behind excalidraw.com. Ideal for diagrams, sketches and visual planning.
note_types.mermaid_description: Create diagrams such as flowcharts, class & sequence diagrams, Gantt charts and many more, using the Mermaid syntax.
note_types.mindmap_description: Organize your thoughts visually or brainstorm ideas.
note_types.markdown_description: Prefer plain text? Write your notes directly in Markdown, keeping them portable and easy to version, while still rendering tables, code blocks and rich formatting in a side-by-side preview.
note_types.spreadsheet_description: A fully-featured spreadsheet for tabular data, with formulas, conditional formatting, data validation, sorting and filtering. Import and export Excel (.xlsx) and CSV files.

collections.title: See your notes from every angle
collections.subtitle: Collections give a group of notes a purpose-built view. Display the very same notes as a calendar, a table, a Kanban board, an interactive map and more, then switch between layouts whenever your needs change.
collections.calendar_description: Organize your personal or professional events using a calendar, with support for all-day and multi-day events. See your events at a glance with the week, month and year views. Easy interaction to add or drag events.
collections.table_description: Display and edit information about notes in a tabular structure, with various column types such as text, number, check boxes, date & time, links and colors and support for relations. Optionally, display the notes within a tree hierarchy inside the table.
collections.board_description: Organize your tasks or project status into a Kanban board with an easy way to create new items and columns and simply changing their status by dragging across the board.
collections.geomap_description: Plan your vacations or mark your points of interest directly on a geographical map using customizable markers. Display recorded GPX tracks to track itineraries.
collections.presentation_description: Organize information into slides and present them in full-screen with smooth transitions. The slides can also be exported to PDF for easy sharing.
collections.dashboard_description: Arrange your notes as draggable, resizable tiles on a grid. Notes becomes live widgets showing their content at a glance; ideal for building a personal homepage or a project overview that brings everything in one place.

import.title: Your notes are never locked in
import.subtitle: Your notes are yours to move freely. Bring them in from other apps, take them back out in open formats, or turn them into polished documents, whenever you like.
import.pillar_import_description: Pull your notes in from other apps with their structure intact, or import standard Markdown, HTML and OPML files.
import.pillar_export_description: Take any note or your entire tree back out in an open, portable format at any time:
import.pillar_pdf_description: Turn your notes into a polished, book-like PDF:
import.pillar_pdf_subtree: An entire subtree, kept in order

ai_integration.title: AI on your terms
ai_integration.subtitle: Optional, and entirely on your terms. AI features are opt-in and disabled by default: your notes are never sent anywhere unless you enable them and connect your own provider.
ai_integration.screenshot_alt: The Trilium AI chat creating notes on your behalf through built-in tools
ai_integration.chat_description: Ask questions and brainstorm in a built-in chat that can draw on the content of your own notes.
ai_integration.tools_title: AI that works with your notes
ai_integration.tools_description: Let an assistant search, create and update notes on your behalf through a set of built-in tools.
ai_integration.providers_description: Connect OpenAI, Anthropic or Google with your own API key, or point Trilium at a self-hosted, OpenAI-compatible model. Expose your notes to external assistants over MCP.

scripting.title: If you can imagine it, you can script it
scripting.subtitle: Trilium isn't just a place to store notes, it's programmable. Write JavaScript that runs in the app or on the server, build your own interface, and automate your workflows, from a one-line tweak to a full custom app.
scripting.screenshot_alt: A Trilium render note displaying an interactive analog clock built with a script
scripting.widgets_description: Add your own buttons, panels and launcher widgets to Trilium's interface using frontend JavaScript.
scripting.backend_description: Run server-side scripts on a schedule or in response to events such as creating or changing a note.
scripting.render_description: Build interactive dashboards and mini-apps that render right inside a note, like the clock shown here.
scripting.api_description: A typed API for notes, branches, attributes and search, available to both frontend and backend scripts.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.mobile_answer: Currently there is no official mobile application. However, if you have a server instance you can access it using a web browser and even install it as a PWA. For Android, there is an unofficial application called TriliumDroid that even works offline (same as a desktop client).
faq.database_answer: All your notes will be stored in an SQLite database in an application folder. Trilium uses a database rather than plain-text files for two reasons: performance and some features such as being placing the same note in multiple places in the tree would be much harder to implement otherwise. To find the application folder, simply go to the About window.
faq.server_question: Do I need a server to use Trilium?
faq.server_answer: No, the server allows access via a web browser and manages the synchronization if you have multiple devices. To get started, it's enough to download the desktop application and start using it.
faq.scaling_question: How well does the application scale with a large amount of notes?
faq.scaling_answer: Depending on usage, the application should be able to handle at least 100,000 notes without an issue. Do note that the sync process can sometimes fail if uploading many large files (1 GB per file) since Trilium is meant more as a knowledge base application rather than a file store (like NextCloud, for example).
faq.network_share_question: Can I share my database over a network drive?
faq.network_share_answer: No, it's generally not a good idea to share a SQLite database over a network drive. Although sometimes it might work, there are chances that the database will get corrupted due to imperfect file locks over a network.
faq.security_answer: By default, notes are not encrypted and can be read directly from the database. Once a note is marked as encrypted, the note is encrypted using AES-128-CBC.
faq.free_answer: Yes, completely. Trilium is free and open-source under the AGPL-3.0 license, with no paid tiers, accounts or feature paywalls. You're free to use, self-host and modify it however you like.
faq.cloud_question: Is there a hosted or cloud version?
faq.cloud_answer: Not an official one yet, though it's something we'd like to offer in the future. For now Trilium is self-hosted: run the desktop app, or host the server yourself. If you'd rather not manage a server, third-party providers such as <0>PikaPods</0> offer managed Trilium hosting.
faq.collaboration_question: Can multiple people collaborate on the same notes?
faq.collaboration_answer: Trilium is designed as a single-user application. You can sync your own notes across all of your devices, and publish read-only notes to the web for others to view, but real-time multi-user editing of the same notes isn't supported.

final_cta.title: Start building your knowledge base
final_cta.description: Organize everything in one open-source app: hierarchical notes, powerful search, and sync to a server you control.

support_us.title: Support us
support_us.financial_donations_description: Trilium is built and maintained with <Link>hundreds of hours of work</Link>. Your support keeps it open-source, improves features, and covers costs such as hosting.
support_us.financial_donations_cta: Consider supporting the main developer (<Link>eliandoran</Link>) of the application via:

contribute.title: Other ways to contribute
contribute.way_translate: Translate the application into your native language via <Link>Weblate</Link>.
contribute.way_community: Interact with the community on <Discussions>GitHub Discussions</Discussions> or on <Matrix>Matrix</Matrix>.
contribute.way_document: Improve the documentation by informing us on gaps in the documentation or contributing guides, FAQs or tutorials.
contribute.way_market: Spread the word: Share Trilium Notes with friends, or on blogs and social media.

404.title: 404: Not Found
404.description: The page you were looking for could not be found. Maybe it was deleted or the URL is incorrect.

download_helper_desktop_windows.description_x64: Compatible with Intel or AMD devices running Windows 10 and 11.
download_helper_desktop_windows.description_arm64: Compatible with ARM devices (e.g. with Qualcomm Snapdragon).

download_helper_desktop_linux.description_x64: For most Linux distributions, compatible with x86_64 architecture.
download_helper_desktop_linux.description_arm64: For ARM-based Linux distributions, compatible with aarch64 architecture.
download_helper_desktop_linux.quick_start: Select an appropriate package format, depending on your distribution:

download_helper_desktop_macos.description_x64: For Intel-based Macs running macOS Monterey or later.
download_helper_desktop_macos.description_arm64: For Apple Silicon Macs such as those with M1 and M2 chips.

download_helper_server_docker.title: Self-hosted using Docker
download_helper_server_docker.description: Easily deploy on Windows, Linux or macOS using a Docker container.

download_helper_server_linux.title: Self-hosted on Linux
download_helper_server_linux.description: Deploy Trilium Notes on your own server or VPS, compatible with most distributions.

download_helper_server_hosted.title: Paid hosting
download_helper_server_hosted.description: Trilium Notes hosted on PikaPods, a paid service for easy access and management. Not directly affiliated with the Trilium team.

resources.title: Resources
resources.icon_packs_intro: Expand the selection of available icons for your notes by using an icon pack. For more information about icon packs, see the <DocumentationLink>official documentation</DocumentationLink>.
